business.headline: 8th Pay Commission to visit Hyderabad, Srinagar, Ladakh: What does this mean?The 8th Pay Commission is taking its discussions beyond Delhi, with visits planned to Hyderabad, Srinagar and Ladakh. But what does this outreach mean for government employees and pensioners awaiting revisions?

Paytm sees strong user trust and high merchant stickiness post PPBL actionBrokerage firms tracking the company said the RBI action on PPBL has limited direct impact on Paytm's core business operations, given the structural separation between the listed entity and the payments bank.

Earning Rs 50,000 a month? The right way to spend, save and investManaging money at Rs 50,000 a month isn't just about covering expenses, it's about making choices that shape your financial future. But are you allocating it in the right way?

From petrol to food, why could everything get costlier togetherA mix of higher oil prices, a weaker rupee and monsoon risks is putting pressure on India's economy. The overlap could lift household costs, limit policy choices and raise stagflation concerns.

GST at record Rs 2.43 lakh crore in April, but domestic demand growth slowsA major part of April's growth came from import-related revenues. GST collected on imports rose sharply by 25.8% to Rs 57,580 crore.

Workplace injury claims jump 31%: Are businesses doing enough?On Labour Day, as conversations around worker safety and rights take centre stage, a pressing question emerges—are businesses truly doing enough to protect their workforce? A recent report suggests concerns are growing.

Even the bank didn't help me: What victims face after reporting digital fraudFor part two of our series on Banking Risks in Digital India, I spoke to victims of digital fraud to understand what they experienced after being scammed. Their stories show how the aftermath can be just as distressing as the fraud itself.

Commercial LPG up by nearly Rs 1,000: Why have prices increased?Commercial LPG cylinder prices have risen again as global oil tensions keep costs elevated. The move shields households for now but raises pressure on restaurants, hotels and small businesses.

Maharashtra Day on May 1: Is the stock market open or closed today?The BSE and NSE are closed on May 1 for Maharashtra Day across key trading segments. Commodity derivatives will reopen in the evening, while traders should account for settlement and liquidity effects.

Commercial, mini LPG prices hiked by Rs 993 and Rs 261, no change in domestic ratesCommercial LPG cylinder prices have surged by Rs 993 to Rs 3,071.50 in Delhi, while domestic LPG, petrol, diesel, and kerosene rates remain unchanged to shield consumers.

Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

Bajaj Finserv announces dividend, reports strong FY26 growthBajaj Finserv reported record FY26 income and profit and announced a special dividend with its Q4 results. The update also marked the completion of Allianz's stake exit, leaving its insurance businesses fully Bajaj-owned.

India-NZ FTA opens doors, but only prepared firms will benefitSiddharth Shankar says the India-New Zealand FTA offers big openings for trade and talent. He argues only firms with strong systems, compliance and execution will truly benefit.

8th Pay Commission: What worker bodies have demanded ahead of salary reviewWorker representatives laid out pay, pension and service demands in the first 8th Pay Commission meeting in Delhi. The staff side said the talks ended positively but pressed for wider consultation and more meetings.

OnEMI Technology IPO: Weak Day 1 response, is it risky for long-term investors?Kissht IPO was subscribed just 0.25 times on Day 1, indicating a slow start. The retail portion saw particularly weak interest, with subscriptions at only 0.06 times.

Where should a 25-year-old invest to build wealth: SIPs, stocks or crypto?At 25, with time on your side, what's the smartest way to invest, i.e., SIPs (Systematic Investment Plans), stocks or crypto? Let's have a look.
Big EPFO update: Minimum pension may rise 7.5 times, ATM withdrawals soonThe government is mulling a sharp increase in the minimum pension under the Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS-95), say sources.

From TDS mismatches to capital gains errors: 3 risks under Income Tax Act 2025The Income Tax Act, 2025 is now in force, and for many companies, the transition seems smooth so far. But could small gaps like TDS mismatches and capital gains errors turn into bigger compliance issues?

Rupee sinks to record low as oil spike and Fed stance pile on pressureThe rupee slipped to a record low against the US dollar as crude oil prices surged and the Federal Reserve stayed hawkish. The move underlines pressure from India's rising import bill, firmer dollar demand and tighter global financial conditions.

entertainment.headline: Ashutosh Gowariker named Festival Director for 57th IFFI in Goa Filmmaker Ashutosh Gowariker has been appointed Festival Director for the 57th International Film Festival of India in Goa. The move links his decades-long association with IFFI to his wider contribution to Indian cinema.

Britney Spears faces driving under influence charge. Can she avoid jail? Britney Spears has been charged in Ventura County with driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs after her March arrest. Prosecutors say a standard plea offer could spare her jail time if she accepts probation, classes and a fine.

Patriot review: Mammootty is invincible, Mohanlal-Fahadh Faasil boost spy dramaRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 Patriot movie review: Director Mahesh Narayanan's Patriot, starring Mammootty, Mohanlal and Fahadh Faasil, is a surveillance thriller that exposes the perils of spyware installed in civilians' tech devices. With intelligent writing and a grounded vision, it is spy cinema done right — minor shortcomings and all.

Siddharth Anand breaks silence on SRK-Deepika's leaked pics from King's shoot Director Siddharth Anand reacted to the leaked photos and videos from King's South Africa schedule. Headlined by Shah Rukh Khan and Deepika Padukone, the film is slated to release on December 24.

Undekhi Season 4 review: Darker, deeper and driven by a trafficking trailRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 Undekhi Season 4 jumps five years ahead as Rinku runs the Atwal empire while Ghosh probes a trafficking case and crosses paths with him again. Teji's death, missing girls and Vikram Saluja's uncertain loyalties deepen the show's moral tension.

Sapne Vs Everyone S2 review: A gritty, grounded look at ambition and its heavy costRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 In a world selling instant success, Sapne Vs Everyone Season 2 reminds us that real dreams come with bruises and betrayals, often amid unbreakable middle-class bonds. Watch it for the vulnerability that hits home — proof that some stories still choose substance over spectacle. Here is our review.

Flicker first look: Anupam Kher says his 551st film feels like a new beginning Anupam Kher has announced his next film Flicker and shared its first poster on social media. While keeping the story under wraps, he called his 551st film a new beginning and a search for something real.

Shekhar Suman, Bharti Singh get relief in 2010 'Rasgulla' joke case The Bombay High Court set aside the 2010 case against Shekhar Suman and Bharti Singh over a Comedy Circus Ka Jadoo joke. The court said the words were used for comic effect and did not show malicious intent to outrage religious sentiments.

What if I die?: Aamir Khan fears plane crash, always writes note to cousin Mansoor Aamir Khan said he writes a note to cousin Mansoor Khan before flights once a film reaches final post-production. The ritual reflects his complete faith in his cousin's instinct and role as a longtime creative support.

Ram Charan completes Peddi shoot, cinematographer shares BTS pics Ram Charan has wrapped his portions for Peddi, with cinematographer Rathnavelu announcing the update on X. The film remains in post-production after a delay, and the makers have yet to reveal a new release date.

Raja Shivaji X review: Riteish earns praise, Salman's cameo steals show The film Raja Shivaji opened in theatres on May 1 with largely positive reactions on X. Several X users praised Riteish Deshmukh's direction and performance, while Salman Khan's cameo became a major talking point.

Raja Shivaji box office: Riteish's film beats Sairat, up for biggest Marathi opening Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji opened with the biggest advance booking ever for a Marathi film. Its pre-release numbers have already overtaken key benchmarks and set up a strong first day.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 43: Ranveer's film enters its last ruling weekend Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has completed 43 days in theatres and is closing in on Baahubali 2's worldwide total. Its sustained run and approaching the 50-day mark underline a rare post-pandemic box office feat for a Hindi film.
South Korean actor Park Dong-bin found dead at 56, probe underway South Korean actor Park Dong-bin died at 56. He was found unresponsive at a restaurant he was preparing to open in Pyeongtaek.

SRK's couldn't even say sorry once: Singer Abhijeet blames actor's ego for fallout Abhijeet Bhattacharya has spoken again about his long-running fallout with Shah Rukh Khan. He said the rift was rooted in ego, self-respect, and the absence of an apology from the superstar.

Actor Evelyn Sharma, husband Tushaan Bhindi split; to co-parent children Actor Evelyn Sharma and dental surgeon Tushaan Bhindi have decided to part ways. They say the split is amicable and that their focus remains on co-parenting their two children.

Salman Khan's superhero film with Raj and DK: See when project is set to start Salman Khan teams up with directors Raj and DK for his first full-fledged superhero film. Although the makers are yet to finalise the title and female lead, read here when the film is set to start.

Salman Khan's cameo in Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji sparks online frenzy. Watch Salman Khan appears as Jiva Mahala in Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji, and his cameo has gone viral online. The brief sword-wielding sequence and standout dialogue have become an early talking point as the film opens in cinemas.

Ek Din review: Sai-Junaid's film is scenic, sweet and strangely lifelessRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 Ek Din review: Sai Pallavi and Junaid Khan's romantic film follows Dinesh and Meera through a one-day love story shaped by memory loss. The film's striking Japan backdrop cannot compensate for thin writing and a romance that never convinces.

general news.headline: Bhagwant Mann drunk in Assembly? Opposition seeks alcohol testTensions erupted in the Punjab Assembly on Labour Day as the Opposition accused Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann of attending the session under the influence of alcohol, with leaders like Pratap Singh Bajwa demanding alcohol and dope tests for all MLAs.

Mamata Banerjee raises EVM swap charge. Is that even possible?West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee asked Trinamool Congress workers to keep a round-the-clock watch on EVM strongrooms in Kolkata after alleging a plot to swap machines during transit. Can EVMs in the strongrooms be swapped? What do the Election Commission protocols say?

Himanta Sarma's 'push back Bangladeshis at night' remark stirs row with DhakaHimanta Biswa Sarma's string of controversial remarks on illegal immigration from Bangladesh seems not to have gone down well with Dhaka. This week, Dhaka summoned the Indian envoy after Sarma remarked on 'pushing back' Bangladeshis, which went viral.

Her phone rang as she was being cremated, revealed UP woman's digital arrest trailMonika, the 28-year-old woman from Uttar Pradesh who died by suicide, had been under digital arrest for months. Her phone kept ringing while she was being cremated, revealing how an online scam pushed her to kill herself.

Patriot review: Mammootty is invincible, Mohanlal-Fahadh Faasil boost spy dramaRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026Patriot movie review: Director Mahesh Narayanan's Patriot, starring Mammootty, Mohanlal and Fahadh Faasil, is a surveillance thriller that exposes the perils of spyware installed in civilians' tech devices. With intelligent writing and a grounded vision, it is spy cinema done right — minor shortcomings and all.

Blow-by-blow account: How TMC dharna over 'EVM manipulation' ended in anti-climaxA late-night row erupted over alleged EVM tampering after the Trinamool Congress claimed suspicious movement at a Kolkata strongroom. Election officials said the activity was part of routine postal ballot segregation even as as Mamata Banerjee asked party workers to maintain round-the-clock vigil outside strongrooms.

Papa don't preach: CBSE's parenting guide raises concerns about overreachCBSE may call it a partnership, but for many parents, this feels like something new. A system that doesn't just teach children, but quietly begins to teach parents how to raise them.

Indian man, stressed over unemployment and father's debt, dies by suicide in USIraganaboyina Chandu, from Kurnool, completed his master's in Chicago and struggled to find a job. Prolonged unemployment caused stress, worsened by concerns about his family's financial burden and his father's work.

Rajiv Rudy's warm welcome for Ashwini Vaishnaw, Jitendra Singh while piloting flightFormer Union Minister and IndiGo pilot Rajiv Pratap Rudy gave Union Ministers Ashwini Vaishnaw, Jitendra Singh and other political figures a special welcome on the Jammu-Delhi flight. A video of the announcement was shared by Northern Railways on X.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Sex harassment claims against JPMorgan exec fabricated by staffer? Report adds twistA former JPMorgan employee identified as Chirayu Rana has accused executive director Lorna Hajdini of sexual abuse in a lawsuit, while the bank says its internal inquiry found no evidence. The dispute has widened into claims of retaliation, with the case drawing scrutiny because the complainant and executive worked as colleagues rather than in a direct reporting line.

Jabalpur tragedy: Woman and son tied to same life jacket, minister breaks downRescuers at Jabalpur boat tragedy site recovered the bodies of a mother and her young son, still locked in their final embrace.

How Socotra island of Yemen became Sikotar Mata in GujaratAncient Indian mariners worshipped a Yemeni island and brought it back home as a deity. The island of Socotra, near East Africa, is believed to have manifested as Sikotar Mata, a sea goddess worshipped in Gujarat even today. Socotra was a vital refuge in the Arabian Sea for maritime traders, over 2,000 km from home.

Did the JPMorgan case remind anyone of the 2004 hit Aitraaz?The JPMorgan case has stark similarities to the plot of Aitraaz, yet is far more complex than the film's clear moral arc starring Akshay Kumar, Priyanka Chopra and Kareena Kapoor Khan.

Rich Dad Poor Dad author exposes Pak's oil shock vs India's remarkable stabilityAcross the border, the picture looked very different. Despite the same global oil shock and crude prices climbing above $120 per barrel, petrol and diesel rates in India stayed largely unchanged across major cities.

Iran president pushes to sack Abbas Araghchi over IRGC tilt in US talks: ReportIran's leadership is facing sharp internal divisions as President Masoud Pezeshkian and Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf push to remove Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, accusing him of sidelining the government and aligning with the Revolutionary Guards during US talks.
6,000 km in 6 days? Amur falcon begins daring flight back to IndiaThe tiny raptor is expected to fly nearly 6,000 km almost non-stop towards the forests along the India-Myanmar border in Manipur.

Enemies spreading rumours, Mojtaba Khamenei healthy, overseeing affairs: IranWord of Mojtaba Khamenei's death was first floated in the second week of March when Donald Trump, in an interview with NBC News, said he did not even know if he was alive. This came after Khamenei did not make any public appearance following joint Israeli and US strikes that killed his father.

TVK office set on fire in Tamil Nadu, CCTV shows 2 men getting off bike at siteTwo miscreants vandalised and set a TVK office on fire in Tamil Nadu's Srirangam ahead of vote counting for the Assembly polls on May 4. CCTV footage surfaced showing two men getting off a bike at the spot and later fleeing.

Is Gen Z actually healthier than Millennials?Experts say Gen Z is more health-aware than millennials were at the same age. They caution that rising stress, burnout and lifestyle risks may be undermining those gains.

Isro finally reveals Gaganyaan astronaut training detailsIsro has outlined how Gaganyatris are being trained through mock-ups and virtual reality simulations for Gaganyaan. The update also shows wider mission readiness work across control, communication and tracking systems.

Nothing, OnePlus, Xiaomi, Realme phone prices increased with immediate effectSmartphone prices in India have gone up from May 1, with Nothing, OnePlus, Realme and Xiaomi revising rates on several models. Updated prices are already visible on official brand websites. Here is the full list of new prices.

More rain coming: May to witness above normal rainfall, confirms IMDThe IMD has forecast above-normal rainfall across India in May 2026, with storms in several regions. The outlook may ease heat stress but raises concerns over flooding and crop damage.

Earning Rs 50,000 a month? The right way to spend, save and investManaging money at Rs 50,000 a month isn't just about covering expenses, it's about making choices that shape your financial future. But are you allocating it in the right way?

sports.headline: I promise you will be on the floor: SRH's Heinrich Klaasen warns fan over filming family IPL 2026: A viral video showed Sunrisers Hyderabad batter Heinrich Klaasen warning a fan to stop filming him and his family over privacy concerns. The exchange stirred fresh debate over privacy, cameras in public spaces and the limits of fandom.

Homework hero: Why Ian Bishop goes beyond stats in IPL commentary IPL 2026: Long before he steps into the IPL commentary box, Ian Bishop is already at work, piecing together stories from research and reportage to bring out the human journeys behind cricket's brightest talents.

F1 returns to Miami with Antonelli on rise and Verstappen's future in doubt Miami GP: Formula 1 returns to Miami with five pressing questions after an unscheduled spring break. Antonelli, Verstappen, Norris and the FIA all arrive under pressure in a season still rapidly taking shape.

India vs Chinese Taipei Live: Sat-Chi 20-12 Chiu-Wang in 3rd game, Indian duo loses second game Thomas Cup 2026 live, India vs Chinese Taipei: Satwik and Chirag lead the tie-breaker after dropping the second game 19-21. Earlier, Lakshya Sen completed a comeback to beat Chinese Taipei's Chou Tien Chen 18-21, 22-20, 21-17 to give India the lead 1-0 in the quarterfinals of the Thomas Cup.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi turns weakness into strength when bowlers err: Ian Bishop IPL 2026: Ian Bishop has argued that 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the temperament and method to take on elite bowling in IPL 2026. As India debut talk builds, his record-breaking run for Rajasthan Royals is turning possibility into pressure.

Will Heinrich Klaasen make South Africa return? SRH star reacts to Pietersen remark Heinrich Klaasen has ruled out a return to international cricket despite calls for a comeback. The South African says his focus is on franchise cricket, Rotterdam in the ETPL and staying mentally fresh.

Trump backs Iran's 2026 World Cup entry in USA after FIFA president's confirmation Donald Trump said he was fine with Iran playing their 2026 World Cup matches in the United States after Gianni Infantino confirmed FIFA's position. The decision keeps a politically charged schedule intact despite Tehran's venue concerns and its delegation's absence from FIFA Congress.

Not young anymore: Bhuvneshwar Kumar plays down Purple Cap milestone focuses on team IPL 2026: Bhuvneshwar Kumar returned with figures of 3/28 against GT, taking his tally to 17 wickets from nine matches and regaining the Purple Cap as the tournament's leading wicket-taker.

After CSK jibe, Shubman Gill makes play bold dig at RCB with Virat Kohli picture Shubman Gill followed Gujarat Titans' win over Royal Challengers Bengaluru with an Instagram post praising Jason Holder and appearing to needle RCB. The post extended the night's playful edge, which also featured banter with Virat Kohli before the two embraced after the match.

RR vs DC Preview: Time for Riyan Parag to step up amidst myriad of controversies RR vs DC Preview: Riyan Parag faces mounting pressure in Jaipur as Rajasthan Royals seek stability, while Delhi Capitals arrive searching for inspiration, clarity and a much-needed spark.

How has Holder filled the Hardik Pandya void for GT? Vikram Solanki explains Jason Holder's all-round display against Royal Challengers Bengaluru powered Gujarat Titans' win and earned warm praise from Vikram Solanki. As Gujarat push for the playoffs, Holder provides balance, experience and communication to fill the gap left by Hardik Pandya's exit in 2024.

IPL Play of the Day: Kohli, Rabada go blow for blow in Ahmedabad's main eventGT vs RCB, IPL Play of the Day: Virat Kohli and Kagiso Rabada traded early blows in Ahmedabad, with the RCB star's powerplay burst met by the GT pacer's sharp tactical adjustment and decisive comeback.

Can India outclass Taipei? All you need to know about Thomas Cup quartersThomas Cup 2026 quarterfinals: India will head into the last-eight tie against Chinese Taipei in Denmark with a slender edge. Lakshya Sen, Ayush Shetty and Kidambi Srikanth will play the singles matches for India on Friday. Here's all you need to know about the big quarterfinal tie.

Riyan Parag vaping row a reminder players need to maintain culture: SangakkaraKumar Sangakkara said Rajasthan Royals' recent controversies have been addressed internally, calling them a reminder for players to uphold team culture ahead of their clash against Delhi Capitals.

Gujarat scratching their heads over middle order woes? Rashid Khan opens upIPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Rashid Khan said Gujarat Titans should not overthink their inconsistent middle order, stressing that T20 cricket exposes every team's weaknesses. On Thursday, Rashid took two wickets as GT beat RCB by four wickets in Ahmedabad.

How fearless Shubman Gill sucked the venom out of RCB pace attackIPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Jos Buttler revealed that Shubman Gill planned a bold counterattack against Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood, as Gujarat Titans beat Royal Challengers Bengaluru by 4 wickets on Thursday in Ahmedabad.

Gujarat Titans take sweet revenge as Bengaluru endure rare off dayIPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Gujarat Titans beat the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by four wickets at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Chasing 156, Shubman Gill's men romped home in 15.5 overs.

Virat Kohli gives Shubman Gill death stare, fiery send-off in Ahmedabad: WatchGT vs RCB, IPL 2026: Virat Kohli brought the intensity with an animated send-off to Shubman Gill, but Gujarat Titans stayed on top after Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder dented RCB in Ahmedabad.

Virat Kohli hits out at umpires after Jason Holder's catch to dismiss Rajat PatidarGT vs RCB, IPL 2026: Virat Kohli was not happy after Rajat Patidar's controversial dismissal against Gujarat Titans, with replays raising questions over Jason Holder's catch and the third umpire's review.

Virat Kohli dominates Kagiso Rabada with boundary blitz, GT pacer answers with revengeIPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Virat Kohli dominated Kagiso Rabada early with a stunning burst of five consecutive boundaries in IPL 2026, but the GT pacer fought back strongly. Rabada had the last laugh by dismissing Kohli for 28.

Was Riyan Parag let off after vaping? Fans call out hypocrisy after escaping banRiyan Parag's dressing-room vaping incident has sparked backlash after the IPL fined him 20 per cent of his match fee, with fans questioning whether the Rajasthan Royals captain was punished lightly.

MCC defends umpires in Angkrish Raghuvanshi Obstructing the Field dismissal callThe MCC defended the umpire's decision to give Angkrish Raghuvanshi out obstructing the field against Lucknow Super Giants, stating it aligns with long-standing laws that penalise wilful changes of running direction.

Indonesia's Thomas Cup exit, a historic first in 68 yearsIndonesia's shock Thomas Cup group-stage exit ends a 68-year run of dominance, exposing a rare crack in badminton's most enduring empire and possibly marking a defining shift in the sport's power balance.

Lungi Ngidi returns to bowling for Delhi capitals after recovering from head injuryIPL 2026: Lungi Ngidi has returned to Delhi Capitals training after the head injury he suffered against Punjab Kings. The footage offers relief, but concussion protocol could still keep him out of the Rajasthan Royals match.

GT vs RCB Highlights: Gujarat beat Bengaluru by 4 wickets in AhmedabadGujarat (GT) vs Bengaluru (RCB) Highlights, IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans earned two crucial points after beating the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by four wickets on Thursday at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Jensi Kanabar, 14, rewrites history books, surpasses Sania Mirza's all-time recordJensi Kanabar became the youngest Indian female to reach a professional singles quarter-final at 14 years and five months, surpassing Sania Mirza's long-standing record with a composed run at the ITF W15 New Delhi.

Cristiano Ronaldo five UCL gesture goes viral after Al Ahli fans' title jibeCristiano Ronaldo answered Al Ahli fans by holding up five fingers after Al Nassr's 2-0 win. The exchange went viral and sharpened the bad blood around a match already clouded by refereeing complaints and on-pitch tension.

Release Hardik Pandya: Mumbai Indians told to reset after 3 mellow years in IPLSimon Doull has urged Mumbai Indians to consider releasing Hardik Pandya if they don't think he will be able to lead the team into the next cycle. Mumbai Indians have had a tough time in IPL 2026. They are rooted to the 9th spot with just two wins in eight games.

Fresh trouble for Riyan Parag? RR captain escapes ban, but women's group seeks probeA women's group has sought an inquiry into visuals purportedly showing Riyan Parag with a vape-like device during an IPL match. It says the incident could normalise a banned product among young viewers and wants accountability.

Riyan Parag escapes ban for vaping during IPL match, fined by BCCIIPL 2026: The Board of Control for Cricket in India has fined Riyan Parag 25% of his match fee for vaping during an IPL match in Mullanpur, with the Rajasthan Royals captain escaping a stricter punishment.

Watch: Venkatesh Prasad's child-like joy in Bengaluru hailstorm, tastes it tooFormer India cricketer Venkatesh Prasad stopped his car during Bengaluru's sudden hailstorm and shared a playful video online. The clip struck a chord as the rare spell cooled the city and briefly turned parts of it white.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
